MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Reopen Discovery To Take The Deposition Of Andrew Martin
Off Calendar Apr-27-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO REOPEN DISCOVERY TO TAKE THE DEPOSITION OF ANDREW MARTIN

Appearances via Zoom:
Michael Vasquez of Wood Smith Henning & Berman LLP (707.559.6710, mvasquez@wshblaw.com) for defendants and moving parties;
Andrew Martin of the Law Office of Andrew Martin (415.755.5599, RMLaw99@gmail.com) for plaintiff and opposing party

Argued and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: granted on the condition that defendants agree that Mr. Martin may remain as the attorney for plaintiff and defendants will not seek to have Mr. Martin disqualified or argue that he should be disqualified. Defendants may signify their assent to this condition by submitting a proposed order that includes language that they so assent. If defendants agree to this condition, there is no legal prejudice to the plaintiff by the granting of this motion and non-expert discovery will now close on May 11, 2026 for the sole purpose of enabling defendants to take the deposition of Mr. Martin. If defendants do not assent to the condition on the granting of this motion, the motion is denied and reasonable monetary sanctions of $1,500 must be paid by defendants to plaintiff no later than May 11, 2026. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/HEK)
